title: hessian/laplacian SIGSEGV when the point is a TENSOR (literal #(...) or (tensor ...))
goal: Second-order AD operators crash on tensor points while first-order ones handle them: (hessian f (tensor 1.0 2.0)) and (laplacian f (tensor 1.0 2.0)) SIGSEGV under -r and AOT, but the SAME calls with (vector 1.0 2.0) return correct values, and gradient/jacobian/divergence/curl all accept tensor points fine. Found by the AD composition oracle (P3): probe cells hess.poly.t2/t3 and lap.poly.t2/t3.
fix_hint: The hessian/laplacian codegen paths (nested reverse mode) likely read the point through the 16-byte tagged-value vector layout; HEAP_SUBTYPE_TENSOR stores homogeneous 8-byte doubles. Mirror whatever tensor-vs-vector header-subtype dispatch the first-order operators use (autodiff_codegen.cpp) when materializing the perturbed points / AD variables.
acceptance: tests/ad_oracle/found/esh0095_hessian_tensor_point_sigsegv.esk prints #((4 2) (2 0)) twice, no crash, -r AND AOT
scripts/run_ad_oracle.sh: ad_oracle_xc_ESH-0095_* files flip XKNOWN->PASS with no other regressions
campaign_payoff: Removes a user-reachable hard crash (tensor is the documented homogeneous point type for AD) and closes 4 xknown cells in the AD oracle matrix.
